title: glasstabs Cheatsheet
text: Call useGlassTabs() somewhere UI using widget.
code: ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   # widgets go here )

title: glasstabs Cheatsheet
code: ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   glassTabsUI(     "main",     glassTabPanel("overview", "Overview", selected = TRUE, h3("Overview")),     glassTabPanel("details", "Details", h3("Details"))   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   active_tab <- glassTabsServer("main") }

title: glasstabs Cheatsheet
text: key rule: use ns() UI, use bare id server.
code: # UI function my_ui <- function(id) {   ns <- NS(id)   tagList(     useGlassTabs(),     glassTabsUI(       ns("tabs"),                          # ns() wraps the id in the UI       glassTabPanel("summary", "Summary", selected = TRUE, h3("Summary")),       glassTabPanel("detail",  "Detail",  h3("Detail"))     )   ) }  # Server function my_server <- function(id) {   moduleServer(id, function(input, output, session) {     active <- glassTabsServer("tabs")    # bare id — no ns() here      observeEvent(active(), {       message("Active tab: ", active())     })   }) }  # App ui     <- fluidPage(my_ui("explorer")) server <- function(input, output, session) my_server("explorer") shinyApp(ui, server)

title: glasstabs Cheatsheet
text: Note: tab value strings must unique — glassTabsUI() error duplicates. Values plain strings; need match labels.
code: # Build panels from data tab_defs <- list(   list(value = "revenue", label = "Revenue"),   list(value = "orders",  label = "Orders"),   list(value = "returns", label = "Returns") )  panels <- lapply(tab_defs, function(t) {   glassTabPanel(t$value, t$label, h3(t$label)) })  ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   do.call(glassTabsUI, c(list("metrics", selected = "orders"), panels)) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   active <- glassTabsServer("metrics") }

title: glasstabs Cheatsheet
code: # Switch active tab updateGlassTabsUI(session, "main", "details")  # Hide or show a tab hideGlassTab(session, "main", "admin") showGlassTab(session, "main", "admin")  # Append or remove a tab at runtime appendGlassTab(   session, "main",   glassTabPanel("compare", "Compare", h3("Compare")),   select = TRUE ) removeGlassTab(session, "main", "compare")

title: glasstabs Cheatsheet
code: glassTabsUI(   "main",   glassTabPanel("a", "A", selected = TRUE, p("A")),   glassTabPanel("b", "B", p("B")),   selected = "a",   wrap = TRUE,   compact = FALSE,      # set TRUE inside dashboard cards for tighter layout   extra_ui = tags$div("Right side UI"),   theme = "light" )

title: glasstabs Cheatsheet
text: Use compact = TRUE embedding glasstabs inside tight layout bs4Dash::bs4Card(). reduces margins, padding, font size widget overflow card.
code: bs4Card(   title = "Explorer", width = 12,   glassTabsUI(     "tabs",     glassTabPanel("a", "A", selected = TRUE, p("Content")),     glassTabPanel("b", "B", p("More content")),     compact = TRUE      # reduced spacing for card context   ) )

title: glasstabs Cheatsheet
text: glassTabsUI() works inside bs4Dash::tabItem(). Place useGlassTabs() dashboardBody() (anywhere first widget — enough). useGlassTabs() needs appear . Inside module renders bs4Dash layout, call module’s UI function.
code: library(shiny) library(bs4Dash)  ui <- bs4DashPage(   header  = bs4DashNavbar(),   sidebar = bs4DashSidebar(     bs4SidebarMenu(       bs4SidebarMenuItem("Explorer", tabName = "explorer", icon = icon("table"))     )   ),   body = bs4DashBody(     useGlassTabs(),                     # place once, anywhere in the body     bs4TabItems(       bs4TabItem(         tabName = "explorer",         bs4Card(           title = "Data explorer",           width = 12,           glassTabsUI(             "s3_tabs",             glassTabPanel("buckets", "Buckets", selected = TRUE,               p("List of S3 buckets here.")             ),             glassTabPanel("objects", "Objects",               p("Object browser here.")             ),             glassTabPanel("preview", "Preview",               p("File preview here.")             )           )         )       )     )   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   active <- glassTabsServer("s3_tabs") }  shinyApp(ui, server)

code: # Tabs glassTabsUI(   "main",   glassTabPanel("a", "A", selected = TRUE, p("A")),   theme = glass_tab_theme(     halo_bg = "rgba(251,191,36,0.15)",     tab_active_text = "#fef3c7"   ) )  # Select widgets glassMultiSelect(   "metric", choices,   theme = glass_select_theme(     mode = "dark",     accent_color = "#38bdf8"   ) )

title: glasstabs Cheatsheet
text: Use glassTabCondition() avoid constructing input key manually:
code: # Instead of: condition = "input['main-active_tab'] === 'details'" conditionalPanel(   condition = glassTabCondition("main", "details"),   p("Only visible on the Details tab.") )  # Inside a module — pass the same id as glassTabsUI(): # glassTabsUI(ns("tabs"), ...) conditionalPanel(   condition = glassTabCondition(NS("mymod")("tabs"), "details"),   p("Only visible on the Details tab.") )

title: glasstabs Cheatsheet
code: glasstabs_news()      # prints changelog to the console

title: glasstabs Cheatsheet
text: useGlassTabs() must called UI. module, use ns("tabs") UI pass bare "tabs" glassTabsServer() — ns("tabs"). Passing namespaced id (containing -) produce warning suggested fix. glassTabPanel() values must unique within glassTabsUI() call — duplicates cause error naming offending value. selected glassTabsUI() refers tab value, label. glassSelectValue() returns reactive function, list. glassMultiSelectValue() returns list selected() style(). glassMultiSelect(), selected use choice values, labels. Use compact = TRUE inside bs4Dash cards tight layout reduce widget spacing. add new JS/CSS behavior development, reinstall load_all() retesting.

title: Getting Started with glasstabs
text: glasstabs provides animated Shiny widgets tab navigation selection controls: Tabs: glass-morphism tab navigation bar sliding halo, spring easing, luminous transfer trace tabs Multi-select filter: dropdown filter animated checkboxes, live search, tag-pill display, three checkbox styles Single-select dropdown: animated single-select input optional search clear support widgets work plain fluidPage() bs4Dash. can used together independently. —

title: Getting Started with glasstabs
code: # From CRAN  install.packages("glasstabs")  # From GitHub pak::pak("YOUR_GITHUB_USERNAME/glasstabs")  # From source devtools::install_local("path/to/glasstabs")

what: The one rule: call useGlassTabs() once
title: Getting Started with glasstabs
text: Every glasstabs app needs exactly one call useGlassTabs() somewhere UI. injects CSS JavaScript proper htmltools dependency.
code: library(shiny) library(glasstabs)  ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   # <-- this is all you need   # ... rest of your UI )

title: Getting Started with glasstabs
text: glassTabPanel() takes unique value, display label, UI content:
code: glassTabPanel("overview", "Overview", selected = TRUE,   h3("Welcome"),   p("This is the overview pane.") )

title: Getting Started with glasstabs
text: Pass panels glassTabsUI() along namespace id:
code: ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   glassTabsUI("nav",     glassTabPanel("overview", "Overview", selected = TRUE,       shiny::h3("Welcome"),       shiny::p("Start here.")     ),     glassTabPanel("analysis", "Analysis",       shiny::h3("Analysis"),       shiny::p("Your charts go here.")     ),     glassTabPanel("settings", "Settings",       shiny::h3("Settings"),       shiny::p("Configuration options.")     )   ) )

title: Getting Started with glasstabs
text: common pattern using one filters drive content inside tab panes. Pass widget extra_ui place glassFilterTags() inside panes show active multi-select filters removable tag pills.
code: choices <- c(North = "north", South = "south", East = "east", West = "west")  ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   glassTabsUI("main",     extra_ui = glassMultiSelect(       inputId             = "region",       choices             = choices,       show_style_switcher = FALSE     ),     glassTabPanel("summary", "Summary", selected = TRUE,       shiny::h3("Summary"),       glassFilterTags("region"),        # tag pills appear here       shiny::uiOutput("summary_text")     ),     glassTabPanel("detail", "Detail",       shiny::h3("Detail"),       glassFilterTags("region"),        # same filter, second pane       shiny::tableOutput("detail_table")     )   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {    selected_regions <- reactive({     input$region %||% unique(unname(choices))   })    output$summary_text <- renderUI({     shiny::p("Showing data for: ",              shiny::strong(paste(selected_regions(), collapse = ", ")))   })    output$detail_table <- renderTable({     data.frame(Region = selected_regions())   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Getting Started with glasstabs
text: widgets default "dark". Switch "light" supply custom theme object — case override need:
code: # Built-in light preset glassTabsUI("nav",    theme = "light", ...) glassMultiSelect("f", theme = "light", ...)  # Custom — one field each glassTabsUI("nav",   theme = glass_tab_theme(halo_bg = "rgba(251,191,36,0.15)"),   ... )  glassMultiSelect("f", choices,   theme = glass_select_theme(accent_color = "#f59e0b") )

title: Getting Started with glasstabs
text: Add wrap = FALSE glass halo positions relative card body rather full-page container. Pair theme = "light":
code: library(bs4Dash) library(glasstabs)  choices <- c(Alpha = "alpha", Beta = "beta", Gamma = "gamma")  ui <- bs4DashPage(   header  = bs4DashNavbar(title = "My App"),   sidebar = bs4DashSidebar(disable = TRUE),   body    = bs4DashBody(     useGlassTabs(),     bs4Card(       title = "Analysis", width = 12,       glassTabsUI("dash",         wrap = FALSE,         theme = "light",         extra_ui = glassMultiSelect(           "f",           choices,           theme = "light",           show_style_switcher = FALSE         ),         glassTabPanel("a", "Overview", selected = TRUE,           shiny::p("Overview content.")         ),         glassTabPanel("b", "Detail",           shiny::p("Detail content.")         )       )     )   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {} shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Single-Select Dropdown with glasstabs
text: glassSelect() animated single-select dropdown Shiny. behaves like standard Shiny input: input$<inputId> stores selected value single character string, NULL nothing selected. supports: optional labels search clear controls built-dark light themes custom theming glass_select_theme() selectable marker styles via check_style server-side updates updateGlassSelect()

title: Single-Select Dropdown with glasstabs
code: library(shiny) library(glasstabs)  fruits <- c(   Apple  = "apple",   Banana = "banana",   Cherry = "cherry",   Mango  = "mango" )  ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   glassSelect("pick", fruits),   verbatimTextOutput("out") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$out <- renderPrint(input$pick) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Single-Select Dropdown with glasstabs
code: # No selection (default) glassSelect("f", fruits)  # Pre-select one value glassSelect("f", fruits, selected = "banana")

title: Single-Select Dropdown with glasstabs
code: glassSelect("f", fruits, label = "Choose a fruit")

title: Single-Select Dropdown with glasstabs
text: glassSelect() supports three selection marker styles: "checkbox" (default) "check-" "filled"
code: glassSelect("f1", fruits, check_style = "checkbox") glassSelect("f2", fruits, check_style = "check-only") glassSelect("f3", fruits, check_style = "filled")

title: Single-Select Dropdown with glasstabs
text: Search enabled default. can also enable clear control. hide search box:
code: glassSelect(   "f",   fruits,   searchable = TRUE,   clearable = TRUE ) glassSelect(   "f",   fruits,   searchable = FALSE )

title: Single-Select Dropdown with glasstabs
text: can prepend explicit “” option: useful want UI distinguish : - selection - deliberate “” selection
code: glassSelect(   "f",   fruits,   include_all = TRUE,   all_choice_label = "All fruits",   all_choice_value = "__all__" )

title: Single-Select Dropdown with glasstabs
text: Read current value directly input$<inputId>:
code: server <- function(input, output, session) {   observe({   message("Selected: ", if (is.null(input$pick)) "NULL" else input$pick) }) }

title: Single-Select Dropdown with glasstabs
text: want small convenience wrapper, use glassSelectValue():
code: server <- function(input, output, session) {   pick <- glassSelectValue(input, "pick")    observe({     print(pick())   }) }

what: Updating choices and selection from the server
title: Single-Select Dropdown with glasstabs
text: updateGlassSelect() can update: - available choices - current selection - selection marker style follows Shiny-style update semantics: choices = NULL leaves choices unchanged selected = NULL leaves selection unchanged selected = character(0) clears selection choices updated without selected, widget keeps current value still exists new set choices.
code: observeEvent(input$filled, {   updateGlassSelect(     session,     "pick",     check_style = "filled"   ) }) ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   actionButton("subset", "Keep first 2 fruits"),   actionButton("banana", "Select banana"),   actionButton("clear", "Clear"),   actionButton("filled", "Use filled style"),   glassSelect("pick", fruits, clearable = TRUE),   verbatimTextOutput("out") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$out <- renderPrint(input$pick)    observeEvent(input$subset, {     updateGlassSelect(       session,       "pick",       choices = fruits[1:2]     )   })    observeEvent(input$banana, {     updateGlassSelect(       session,       "pick",       selected = "banana"     )   })    observeEvent(input$clear, {     updateGlassSelect(       session,       "pick",       selected = character(0)     )   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

code: glassSelect("f", fruits, theme = "dark") glassSelect("f", fruits, theme = "light")

code: glassSelect(   "f",   fruits,   theme = glass_select_theme(     mode = "dark",     accent_color = "#f59e0b"   ) )  glassSelect(   "f",   fruits,   theme = glass_select_theme(     bg_color = "#1a0a2e",     border_color = "#a855f7",     text_color = "#ede9fe",     accent_color = "#a855f7"   ) )   #other themes glassSelect(   "f",   fruits,   theme = glass_select_theme(     mode = "light",     accent_color = "#2563eb"   ) )  #more themes glassSelect(   "f",   fruits,   theme = glass_select_theme(     mode = "light",     bg_color = "#ffffff",     border_color = "rgba(0,0,0,0.15)",     text_color = "#111111",     accent_color = "#111111",     label_color = "#111111"   ) )

text: glassSelect() can used without tab widget. need useGlassTabs() load package CSS JavaScript assets:
code: ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   glassSelect("region", c(North = "North", South = "South", East = "East")),   verbatimTextOutput("out") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$out <- renderPrint(input$region) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Multi-Select Filter with glasstabs
text: glassMultiSelect() animated multi-select dropdown Shiny. can used inside glassTabsUI() layout completely Shiny page. widget behaves like standard Shiny input: input$<inputId> stores selected values, input$<inputId>_style stores active indicator style. also supports server-side updates updateGlassMultiSelect().

title: Multi-Select Filter with glasstabs
code: library(shiny) library(glasstabs)  fruits <- c(Apple = "apple",              Banana = "banana",              Cherry = "cherry",             Mango  = "mango",              Peach  = "peach")  ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   glassMultiSelect("pick", fruits),   verbatimTextOutput("out") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$out <- renderPrint(input$pick) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Multi-Select Filter with glasstabs
code: # All selected (default) glassMultiSelect("f", fruits)  # Subset pre-selected glassMultiSelect("f", fruits, selected = c("apple", "cherry"))  # Nothing pre-selected glassMultiSelect("f", fruits, selected = character(0))

title: Multi-Select Filter with glasstabs
text: Three indicator styles available via check_style: default, style switcher row shown inside dropdown user can change style runtime. Hide show_style_switcher = FALSE:
code: # Bordered box + animated tick (default) glassMultiSelect("f", fruits, check_style = "checkbox")  # Tick only — minimal, no box border glassMultiSelect("f", fruits, check_style = "check-only")  # Solid coloured box — each option gets its own hue glassMultiSelect("f", fruits, check_style = "filled") glassMultiSelect("f", fruits,   check_style         = "check-only",   show_style_switcher = FALSE          # lock the style silently )

what: Custom hues for the filled style
title: Multi-Select Filter with glasstabs
text: check_style = "filled", hues auto-assigned evenly around colour wheel. Override named integer vector HSL hue angles (0–360):
code: glassMultiSelect("f", fruits,   check_style = "filled",   hues = c(apple = 0, banana = 50, cherry = 340, mango = 30, peach = 20) )

title: Multi-Select Filter with glasstabs
text: three interactive chrome elements can toggled independently. Defaults TRUE:
code: glassMultiSelect("f", fruits,   show_style_switcher = FALSE,   # hide the Check / Box / Fill row   show_select_all     = FALSE,   # hide the "Select all" row   show_clear_all      = FALSE    # hide the "Clear all" footer link )

title: Multi-Select Filter with glasstabs
text: Place glassFilterTags() container anywhere page show active selections removable tag pills. JS engine keeps sync automatically — clicking × tag deselects option. can place multiple glassFilterTags("cat") containers filter — stay sync.
code: ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   glassMultiSelect("cat", fruits, show_style_switcher = FALSE),   glassFilterTags("cat")    # can be placed anywhere, even in a different tab pane )

title: Multi-Select Filter with glasstabs
text: Read selection directly input$<inputId> style input$<inputId>_style:
code: server <- function(input, output, session) {    # Direct access   observe({     message("Selected: ", paste(input$pick, collapse = ", "))     message("Style: ",    input$pick_style)   }) }

title: Multi-Select Filter with glasstabs
text: want small convenience wrapper, use glassMultiSelectValue():
code: server <- function(input, output, session) {   ms <- glassMultiSelectValue(input, "pick")    observe({     message("Selected: ", paste(ms$selected(), collapse = ", "))     message("Style: ", ms$style())   }) }

what: Updating choices and selection from the server
title: Multi-Select Filter with glasstabs
text: updateGlassMultiSelect() can update: - available choices - current selection - active style follows Shiny-style update semantics: choices = NULL leaves choices unchanged selected = NULL leaves selection unchanged selected = character(0) clears selection choices updated without selected, widget keeps intersection current selection new choice set.
code: ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   actionButton("subset", "Keep first 3 fruits"),   actionButton("pick2", "Select 2 fruits"),   actionButton("clear", "Clear"),   actionButton("fill", "Switch to filled style"),   glassMultiSelect("pick", fruits),   verbatimTextOutput("out") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$out <- renderPrint(input$pick)    observeEvent(input$subset, {     updateGlassMultiSelect(       session,       "pick",       choices = fruits[1:3]     )   })    observeEvent(input$pick2, {     updateGlassMultiSelect(       session,       "pick",       selected = c("apple", "cherry")     )   })    observeEvent(input$clear, {     updateGlassMultiSelect(       session,       "pick",       selected = character(0)     )   })    observeEvent(input$fill, {     updateGlassMultiSelect(       session,       "pick",       check_style = "filled"     )   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Multi-Select Filter with glasstabs
code: glassMultiSelect("f", fruits, theme = "dark")   # default glassMultiSelect("f", fruits, theme = "light")  # white panel, dark text

title: Multi-Select Filter with glasstabs
text: Supply colors want change:
code: # One field — accent colour only glassMultiSelect("f", fruits,   theme = glass_select_theme(accent_color = "#f59e0b") )  # Two fields glassMultiSelect("f", fruits,   theme = glass_select_theme(     text_color   = "#1e293b",     accent_color = "#2563eb"   ) )  # All four fields glassMultiSelect("f", fruits,   theme = glass_select_theme(     bg_color     = "#1a0a2e",     border_color = "#a855f7",     text_color   = "#ede9fe",     accent_color = "#a855f7"   ) )

title: Multi-Select Filter with glasstabs
text: glassMultiSelect() fully independent tab widget. need useGlassTabs() CSS JS:
code: library(shiny) library(glasstabs)  sales <- data.frame(   region  = c("North","North","South","South","East","West"),   product = c("Alpha","Beta","Alpha","Gamma","Beta","Alpha"),   revenue = c(42000, 31000, 27000, 55000, 38000, 61000) )  ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   glassMultiSelect("region",  c(North="North", South="South",                                 East="East",   West="West"),                    show_style_switcher = FALSE),   glassMultiSelect("product", c(Alpha="Alpha", Beta="Beta", Gamma="Gamma"),                    show_style_switcher = FALSE,                    check_style = "check-only"),   glassFilterTags("region"),   glassFilterTags("product"),   tableOutput("tbl") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   filtered <- reactive({     sales[sales$region  %in% (input$region  %||% unique(sales$region)) &           sales$product %in% (input$product %||% unique(sales$product)), ]   })   output$tbl <- renderTable(filtered()) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Multi-Select Filter with glasstabs
text: glassMultiSelect() scoped inputId works independently. one dropdown open automatically floats others:
code: ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   glassMultiSelect("a", c(X = "x", Y = "y", Z = "z")),   glassMultiSelect("b", c(P = "p", Q = "q", R = "r"),                    check_style = "filled",                    show_style_switcher = FALSE) )

title: Animated Tabs with glasstabs
text: glassTabsUI() provides animated glass-morphism tab navigation bar Shiny. active tab tracked sliding glass halo spring easing, luminous transfer trace sweeps across intermediate tabs navigation.

title: Animated Tabs with glasstabs
text: Three functions work together: useGlassTabs() — injects CSS JS , anywhere page UI glassTabsUI() — renders full tab bar content area glassTabPanel() — defines one tab button associated content pane
code: library(shiny) library(glasstabs)  ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   glassTabsUI("nav",     glassTabPanel("overview", "Overview", selected = TRUE,       h3("Overview"),       p("This pane is shown first.")     ),     glassTabPanel("details", "Details",       h3("Details"),       p("Switch to this tab using the button above.")     ),     glassTabPanel("settings", "Settings",       h3("Settings"),       p("A third tab.")     )   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {}  shinyApp(ui, server)

what: Reading the active tab in the server
title: Animated Tabs with glasstabs
text: active tab value pushed Shiny automatically every click via Shiny.setInputValue. Access input[["<id>-active_tab"]]:
code: server <- function(input, output, session) {   observe({     req(input[["nav-active_tab"]])     message("Active tab: ", input[["nav-active_tab"]])   })    # Or use the convenience wrapper   active <- glassTabsServer("nav")   observe(message("Active: ", active())) }

title: Animated Tabs with glasstabs
text: Arrow keys move tabs focus inside widget — extra code needed.

what: Placing a filter widget beside the tabs
title: Animated Tabs with glasstabs
text: Pass UI element extra_ui place right tab bar. common use glassMultiSelect() filter:
code: choices <- c(Alpha = "alpha", Beta = "beta", Gamma = "gamma")  ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   glassTabsUI("nav",     extra_ui = glassMultiSelect("cat", choices, show_style_switcher = FALSE),     glassTabPanel("a", "Tab A", selected = TRUE,       p("Content A"),       glassFilterTags("cat")   # tag pills synced to the filter     ),     glassTabPanel("b", "Tab B",       p("Content B"),       glassFilterTags("cat")     )   ) )

title: Animated Tabs with glasstabs
code: # Dark (default) glassTabsUI("nav", theme = "dark", ...)  # Light — suits white page backgrounds and bs4Dash cards glassTabsUI("nav", theme = "light", ...)

title: Animated Tabs with glasstabs
text: Supply values want change — everything else falls back dark preset: eight handles available glass_tab_theme():
code: # Change only the halo colour glassTabsUI("nav",   theme = glass_tab_theme(     halo_bg         = "rgba(251,191,36,0.18)",     halo_border     = "rgba(251,191,36,0.40)",     tab_active_text = "#fef3c7"   ),   glassTabPanel("a", "Tab", selected = TRUE, p("Content")) )

title: Animated Tabs with glasstabs
text: Set wrap = FALSE embedding inside bs4Card bs4Box — card body already provides constrained container. Pair theme = "light" match card’s white background:
code: library(bs4Dash) library(glasstabs)  ui <- bs4DashPage(   header  = bs4DashNavbar(title = "My App"),   sidebar = bs4DashSidebar(disable = TRUE),   body    = bs4DashBody(     useGlassTabs(),     bs4Card(       title = "Analysis", width = 12,       glassTabsUI("dash",         wrap     = FALSE,         theme    = "light",         extra_ui = glassMultiSelect("f", choices, theme = "light",                                     show_style_switcher = FALSE),         glassTabPanel("a", "Overview", selected = TRUE, p("Overview content")),         glassTabPanel("b", "Details",  p("Detail content"))       )     )   ) )

what: Multiple tab widgets on one page
title: Animated Tabs with glasstabs
text: glassTabsUI() instance scoped id, multiple widgets work fully independently:
code: ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),    # only needed once per page   glassTabsUI("widget1",     glassTabPanel("a", "One-A", selected = TRUE, p("Widget 1, pane A")),     glassTabPanel("b", "One-B", p("Widget 1, pane B"))   ),   glassTabsUI("widget2",     glassTabPanel("x", "Two-X", selected = TRUE, p("Widget 2, pane X")),     glassTabPanel("y", "Two-Y", p("Widget 2, pane Y"))   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   observe(message("Widget 1 active: ", input[["widget1-active_tab"]]))   observe(message("Widget 2 active: ", input[["widget2-active_tab"]])) }

text: glasstabs provides animated Shiny widgets built around glass-morphism aesthetic: glassTabsUI() — animated tab bar sliding glass halo follows active tab glassMultiSelect() — multi-select dropdown three selection styles, live search, tag-pill syncing, server-side update support glassSelect() — animated single-select dropdown optional search, clear support, theming, selection styles, server-side update support widgets self-contained, fully themeable, work plain fluidPage(), bs4DashPage(), Shiny page wrapper. Full documentation: https://prigasg.github.io/glasstabs/

title: glasstabs — Animated Shiny Widgets
code: # From CRAN install.packages("glasstabs")  # From GitHub (development version) pak::pak("prigasG/glasstabs") # or devtools::install_github("prigasG/glasstabs")

title: glasstabs — Animated Shiny Widgets
text: Note: useGlassTabs() must called somewhere UI glassTabsUI() glassMultiSelect() call. injects shared CSS JavaScript properly deduplicated htmltools dependency.
code: library(shiny) library(glasstabs)  ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   glassTabsUI(     "main",     glassTabPanel(       "t1", "Overview", selected = TRUE,       h3("Overview"),       p("Content here."),       glassFilterTags("cat")     ),     glassTabPanel(       "t2", "Details",       h3("Details"),       p("More content."),       glassFilterTags("cat")     ),     extra_ui = glassMultiSelect(       "cat",       c(A = "a", B = "b", C = "c"),       show_style_switcher = FALSE     )   ),   verbatimTextOutput("info") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   tabs <- glassTabsServer("main")   filt <- glassMultiSelectValue(input, "cat")    output$info <- renderPrint({     list(       active_tab = tabs(),       selected = filt$selected(),       style = filt$style()     )   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: glasstabs — Animated Shiny Widgets
text: Use glassTabCondition() avoid constructing input key manually:
code: # Instead of: condition = "input['main-active_tab'] === 'details'" conditionalPanel(   condition = glassTabCondition("main", "details"),   p("Only visible on the Details tab.") )  # Inside a module — pass the same id as glassTabsUI(): # glassTabsUI(ns("tabs"), ...) # conditionalPanel(condition = glassTabCondition(ns("tabs"), "details"), ...)

title: glasstabs — Animated Shiny Widgets
text: Note: default, glassMultiSelect() starts choices selected.
code: library(shiny) library(glasstabs)  choices <- c(   Apple  = "apple",   Banana = "banana",   Cherry = "cherry" )  ui <- fluidPage(   useGlassTabs(),   glassMultiSelect("fruit", choices),   glassFilterTags("fruit"),   verbatimTextOutput("out") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$out <- renderPrint(input$fruit) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: glasstabs — Animated Shiny Widgets
code: server <- function(input, output, session) {   # Switch to a tab programmatically   observeEvent(input$next_btn, {     updateGlassTabsUI(session, "tabs", selected = "details")   })    # Show/hide tabs conditionally   observeEvent(input$is_admin, {     if (input$is_admin) showGlassTab(session, "tabs", "admin")     else                hideGlassTab(session, "tabs", "admin")   }, ignoreInit = FALSE)    # Add and remove tabs dynamically   observeEvent(input$add_tab, {     appendGlassTab(session, "tabs",       glassTabPanel("dynamic", "Dynamic", p("Added at runtime")),       select = TRUE     )   })    observeEvent(input$remove_tab, {     removeGlassTab(session, "tabs", "dynamic")   }) }

text: inputId Shiny input id. choices Named unnamed character vector choices. selected Initially selected values. Defaults choices NULL. label Optional field label shown widget. placeholder Trigger label nothing selected. all_label Label shown choices selected. check_style One "checkbox" (default), "check-", "filled". show_style_switcher Show Check / Box / Fill switcher row inside dropdown? Default TRUE. show_select_all Show "Select " row? Default TRUE. show_clear_all Show "Clear " footer link? Default TRUE. theme Color theme. One "dark" (default) "light", glass_select_theme() object. hues Optional named integer vector HSL hue angles (0 360) "filled" style. Auto-assigned NULL.

title: Server logic for glass tabs — glassTabsServer
text: glassTabsServer() follows calling convention Shiny module server functions: pass bare module id, namespaced one. Inside parent module, pair glassTabsUI(ns("tabs"), ...) UI, call glassTabsServer("tabs") (without ns()) server.

title: Server logic for glass tabs — glassTabsServer
code: # --- Standalone app --- if (interactive()) {   library(shiny)   ui <- fluidPage(     useGlassTabs(),     glassTabsUI(       "tabs",       glassTabPanel("a", "A", p("Tab A"), selected = TRUE),       glassTabPanel("b", "B", p("Tab B"))     )   )   server <- function(input, output, session) {     active <- glassTabsServer("tabs")     observe(print(active()))   }   shinyApp(ui, server) }  # --- Bookmarking --- if (interactive()) {   library(shiny)   ui <- function(request) {     fluidPage(       useGlassTabs(),       bookmarkButton(),       glassTabsUI(         "tabs",         glassTabPanel("a", "A", p("Tab A"), selected = TRUE),         glassTabPanel("b", "B", p("Tab B"))       )     )   }   server <- function(input, output, session) {     active <- glassTabsServer("tabs", bookmark = TRUE)   }   shinyApp(ui, server, enableBookmarking = "url") }  # --- Inside a Shiny module --- # UI side: use ns() to namespace the widget id my_module_ui <- function(id) {   ns <- shiny::NS(id)   shiny::tagList(     useGlassTabs(),     glassTabsUI(       ns("tabs"),                          # <-- ns() wraps the id here       glassTabPanel("a", "A", p("Tab A"), selected = TRUE),       glassTabPanel("b", "B", p("Tab B"))     )   ) }  # Server side: pass the bare id — NOT ns("tabs") my_module_server <- function(id) {   shiny::moduleServer(id, function(input, output, session) {     active <- glassTabsServer("tabs")     # <-- bare id, no ns()     shiny::observe(print(active()))   }) }

code: glasstabs_news() #>                         Changes in version 0.3.1                         #>  #> New features #>  #>   - Added glassTabCondition(id, value) — generates the correct #>     JavaScript condition string for [shiny::conditionalPanel()] without #>     needing to remember the input[["id-active_tab"]] key pattern. #>   - Added glasstabs_news() — prints the package changelog to the R #>     console. #>   - Added ROADMAP.md tracking planned features and known limitations #>     (not shipped in the package). #>  #> Improvements #>  #>   - Error messages across all functions are now actionable: they name #>     the bad argument class, show what was received, and suggest the #>     correct fix. #>   - glass_tab_theme() and glass_select_theme() now have full @examples #>     with interactive app snippets. #>   - .gt-container no longer forces max-width:960px or margin:48px auto — #>     the widget now behaves like a normal block element inside Shiny #>     layouts. #>   - Light-mode halo box-shadow replaced with a soft blue-tinted shadow #>     via the --gt-halo-shadow CSS variable, replacing the harsh dark drop #>     shadow. #>  #>                         Changes in version 0.3.0                         #>  #> New features #>  #> Tab widget #>  #>   - Added runGlassExample() to launch any of the built-in example apps #>     directly from the R console (runGlassExample("smoke-test"), etc.). #>   - Added icon argument to glassTabPanel() — accepts any #>     htmltools-compatible tag (e.g. shiny::icon("table")). Renders with #>     gt-tab-icon / gt-tab-label spans so icon and text can be styled #>     independently. #>   - Added disableGlassTab() and enableGlassTab() — gray out a tab #>     without hiding it. Completes the visibility/disabled/removal trio #>     alongside showGlassTab() / hideGlassTab(). #>   - Added updateGlassTabBadge() — set a numeric count badge on any tab #>     button. Pass count = 0 or count = NA to hide the badge. Values #>     above 99 display as "99+". #>   - glassTabsServer() now integrates with Shiny's URL bookmarking: the #>     active tab is automatically saved and restored when #>     enableBookmarking = "url" is set on the app. Opt out per widget with #>     bookmark = FALSE. #>   - Added glassTabsOutput() / renderGlassTabs() for server-driven tab #>     rendering. The JavaScript engine re-initialises automatically after #>     each render via a scoped shiny:value listener, so no manual #>     glasstabs_reinit calls are needed. #>  #> Improvements #>  #>   - glassTabsUI() now errors early on duplicate glassTabPanel() values, #>     reporting the offending value(s) by name. #>   - glassTabsUI() gains a compact = FALSE argument. Setting compact = #>     TRUE applies the .gt-compact CSS modifier which reduces margins, #>     tab-link padding, font size, and content area padding — ideal for #>     embedding inside bs4Dash cards or any tight dashboard layout. #>   - glassTabsServer() emits a warning() when the id argument contains #>     "-", which is the most common sign that ns("tabs") was passed #>     instead of the bare "tabs" id. The warning includes corrective #>     guidance. #>   - CSS: added .gt-tab-disabled, .gt-tab-icon, .gt-tab-label, and #>     .gt-tab-badge rules. Badges adapt their background to the active #>     halo colour via the existing CSS custom property. #>   - CSS: all color values are now consumed via var(--gt-xxx, fallback) #>     instead of being hardcoded — this was the root cause of light theme #>     appearing invisible. Light theme defaults were also strengthened for #>     better contrast. #>   - CSS: dropdown active-state z-index raised to 9000/9001 so glasstabs #>     dropdowns render above bs4Dash card stacking contexts. #>   - shiny:value listener limits bootAll re-initialisation to outputs #>     that actually contain glasstabs elements, so ordinary Shiny outputs #>     are unaffected. #>   - Added inst/cheatsheet/glasstabs-cheatsheet.tex — a printable #>     four-column LaTeX reference card covering the full API, module #>     pattern, bs4Dash integration, theming, and common gotchas. #>  #> Documentation #>  #>   - Cheatsheet vignette (vignettes/cheatsheet.Rmd) extended with new #>     sections: "Tabs inside a Shiny module", "Dynamic tab values and #>     selected", "Tabs inside bs4Dash", and updated common gotchas. #>   - All new functions have full roxygen docs, @examples, and #>     cross-links. #>  #>                         Changes in version 0.2.1                         #>  #> New features #>  #> Tab widget #>  #>   - Added updateGlassTabsUI() for programmatic tab switching from the #>     server. #>   - Added showGlassTab() and hideGlassTab() for dynamically showing and #>     hiding tabs at runtime. #>   - Added appendGlassTab() and removeGlassTab() for adding and removing #>     tabs at runtime. #>  #> Improvements #>  #>   - glassTabsUI() now validates selected against the available #>     glassTabPanel() values and errors early on invalid tab ids. #>   - The tab widget now reports its initial active tab to Shiny #>     immediately after session startup, so glassTabsServer() is populated #>     on first render. #>   - Added ARIA roles and selected-state attributes to tab navigation #>     markup. #>   - Improved tab re-initialization and transition handling for dynamic #>     tab changes, including show/hide, append/remove, and rapid #>     successive updates. #>   - Improved halo and transfer-trace positioning after runtime tab #>     layout changes. #>  #> Bug fixes #>  #>   - Opening a dropdown now closes any other open glasstabs dropdown, #>     fixing a visual stacking issue where multiple dropdowns appeared #>     simultaneously. #>  #>   - Improved widget lifecycle in dynamic UI (renderUI(), #>     conditionalPanel()). Widgets initialize more reliably and clean up #>     properly when removed. #>  #>   - Widgets now emit their initial value to Shiny immediately after #>     rendering, fixing timing issues with conditionalPanel() and dynamic #>     UI. #>  #> Internal improvements #>  #>   - JavaScript engine refactored to use internal state instead of DOM #>     scanning. No changes to the R API — all existing code works without #>     modification. #>  #>   - Added scroll containers for long option lists, debounced search, and #>     .gt-loading / .gt-disabled CSS utility classes. #>  #> Documentation #>  #>   - Added a dedicated cheatsheet vignette for common widget patterns and #>     server-side helpers. #>   - Added a smoke-test example app covering programmatic tab switching, #>     dynamic tab visibility, runtime tab insertion/removal, and rapid #>     interaction paths. #>   - Expanded README and pkgdown navigation to surface the new #>     server-side tab controls and cheatsheet more clearly. #>  #>                         Changes in version 0.1.1                         #>  #> New features #>  #> Single-select widget #>  #>   - Added glassSelect(), an animated single-select dropdown for Shiny. #>   - Added updateGlassSelect() for server-side updates to choices and #>     selection. #>   - Added glassSelectValue() as a convenience reactive helper for #>     reading the selected value. #>  #> Multi-select widget #>  #>   - Added updateGlassMultiSelect() for server-side updates to choices, #>     selection, and checkbox style. #>   - Added glassMultiSelectValue() as a convenience reactive helper for #>     reading selected values and active style. #>   - Added optional label support to glassMultiSelect(). #>   - Added configurable all_label support to control the trigger text #>     shown when all choices are selected. #>  #> Improvements #>  #>   - Aligned glassSelect() and glassMultiSelect() more closely with #>     standard Shiny input behavior. #>   - Added client-side input bindings so select widgets now support #>     session$sendInputMessage() update patterns. #>   - Preserved current glassMultiSelect() default behavior where selected #>     = NULL initializes all choices as selected. #>   - Improved choice normalization so named choices retain display labels #>     correctly. #>   - Improved hue normalization for the "filled" multi-select style. #>   - Updated vignettes and examples to show direct Shiny input usage and #>     server-side update patterns. #>  #> Documentation #>  #>   - Added a new vignette for glassSelect(). #>   - Updated the getting started vignette to include glassSelect(). #>   - Updated the multi-select vignette to document #>     updateGlassMultiSelect() and glassMultiSelectValue(). #>  #> Internal changes #>  #>   - Added tests for glassSelect(), updateGlassSelect(), and #>     glassSelectValue(). #>   - Expanded tests for glassMultiSelect() server-side updates and helper #>     utilities. #>  #>                         Changes in version 0.1.0                         #>  #> Initial release. #>  #> New features #>  #> Tab widget #>  #>   - glassTabsUI() — animated glassmorphism-style tab navigation with a #>     sliding glass halo and transfer trace between tabs. #>   - glassTabPanel() — defines a single tab button and its content pane. #>   - glassTabsServer() — reactive helper that tracks the active tab #>     value. #>   - glass_tab_theme() — custom color theme for glassTabsUI() with eight #>     independent handles: tab_text, tab_active_text, halo_bg, #>     halo_border, content_bg, content_border, card_bg, card_text. #>   - Built-in "dark" (default) and "light" presets for glassTabsUI(). #>   - Keyboard navigation using the Left and Right arrow keys. #>   - Full bs4Dash compatibility via wrap = FALSE. #>  #> Multi-select filter widget #>  #>   - glassMultiSelect() — dropdown filter with live search, select-all #>     with indeterminate state, and three checkbox indicator styles: #>     "checkbox", "check-only", and "filled". #>   - glassFilterTags() — tag-pill display area that stays in sync with a #>     glassMultiSelect() automatically via JavaScript; clicking × on a #>     pill deselects that option. #>   - glass_select_theme() — custom color theme with four handles: #>     bg_color, border_color, text_color, accent_color. #>   - Built-in "dark" and "light" presets for glassMultiSelect(). #>   - show_style_switcher, show_select_all, show_clear_all flags to hide #>     UI chrome (all default TRUE). #>   - Per-option HSL hue angles for the "filled" style via the hues #>     argument (auto-assigned if omitted). #>  #> General #>  #>   - useGlassTabs() — injects CSS and JavaScript via #>     htmltools::htmlDependency, which is deduplicated automatically by #>     Shiny. #>   - All theming uses pre-computed CSS variables (no color-mix()), #>     ensuring compatibility with Shiny's embedded browser. #>   - Multiple instances of either widget on the same page work #>     independently; each instance is scoped to its own id.

title: glasstabs 0.3.1
text: Added glassTabCondition(id, value) — generates correct JavaScript condition string [shiny::conditionalPanel()] without needing remember input[["id-active_tab"]] key pattern. Added glasstabs_news() — prints package changelog R console. Added ROADMAP.md tracking planned features known limitations (shipped package).

title: glasstabs 0.3.1
text: Error messages across functions now actionable: name bad argument class, show received, suggest correct fix. glass_tab_theme() glass_select_theme() now full @examples interactive app snippets. .gt-container longer forces max-width:960px margin:48px auto — widget now behaves like normal block element inside Shiny layouts. Light-mode halo box-shadow replaced soft blue-tinted shadow via --gt-halo-shadow CSS variable, replacing harsh dark drop shadow.

title: glasstabs 0.3.0
text: Added runGlassExample() launch built-example apps directly R console (runGlassExample("smoke-test"), etc.). Added icon argument glassTabPanel() — accepts htmltools-compatible tag (e.g. shiny::icon("table")). Renders gt-tab-icon / gt-tab-label spans icon text can styled independently. Added disableGlassTab() enableGlassTab() — gray tab without hiding . Completes visibility/disabled/removal trio alongside showGlassTab() / hideGlassTab(). Added updateGlassTabBadge() — set numeric count badge tab button. Pass count = 0 count = NA hide badge. Values 99 display "99+". glassTabsServer() now integrates Shiny’s URL bookmarking: active tab automatically saved restored enableBookmarking = "url" set app. Opt per widget bookmark = FALSE. Added glassTabsOutput() / renderGlassTabs() server-driven tab rendering. JavaScript engine re-initialises automatically render via scoped shiny:value listener, manual glasstabs_reinit calls needed.

title: glasstabs 0.3.0
text: glassTabsUI() now errors early duplicate glassTabPanel() values, reporting offending value(s) name. glassTabsUI() gains compact = FALSE argument. Setting compact = TRUE applies .gt-compact CSS modifier reduces margins, tab-link padding, font size, content area padding — ideal embedding inside bs4Dash cards tight dashboard layout. glassTabsServer() emits warning() id argument contains "-", common sign ns("tabs") passed instead bare "tabs" id. warning includes corrective guidance. CSS: added .gt-tab-disabled, .gt-tab-icon, .gt-tab-label, .gt-tab-badge rules. Badges adapt background active halo colour via existing CSS custom property. CSS: color values now consumed via var(--gt-xxx, fallback) instead hardcoded — root cause light theme appearing invisible. Light theme defaults also strengthened better contrast. CSS: dropdown active-state z-index raised 9000/9001 glasstabs dropdowns render bs4Dash card stacking contexts. shiny:value listener limits bootAll re-initialisation outputs actually contain glasstabs elements, ordinary Shiny outputs unaffected. Added inst/cheatsheet/glasstabs-cheatsheet.tex — printable four-column LaTeX reference card covering full API, module pattern, bs4Dash integration, theming, common gotchas.

title: glasstabs 0.3.0
text: Cheatsheet vignette (vignettes/cheatsheet.Rmd) extended new sections: “Tabs inside Shiny module”, “Dynamic tab values selected”, “Tabs inside bs4Dash”, updated common gotchas. new functions full roxygen docs, @examples, cross-links.

title: glasstabs 0.2.1

title: glasstabs 0.2.1
text: Added updateGlassTabsUI() programmatic tab switching server. Added showGlassTab() hideGlassTab() dynamically showing hiding tabs runtime. Added appendGlassTab() removeGlassTab() adding removing tabs runtime.

title: glasstabs 0.2.1
text: glassTabsUI() now validates selected available glassTabPanel() values errors early invalid tab ids. tab widget now reports initial active tab Shiny immediately session startup, glassTabsServer() populated first render. Added ARIA roles selected-state attributes tab navigation markup. Improved tab re-initialization transition handling dynamic tab changes, including show/hide, append/remove, rapid successive updates. Improved halo transfer-trace positioning runtime tab layout changes.

title: glasstabs 0.2.1
text: Opening dropdown now closes open glasstabs dropdown, fixing visual stacking issue multiple dropdowns appeared simultaneously. Improved widget lifecycle dynamic UI (renderUI(), conditionalPanel()). Widgets initialize reliably clean properly removed. Widgets now emit initial value Shiny immediately rendering, fixing timing issues conditionalPanel() dynamic UI.

title: glasstabs 0.2.1
text: JavaScript engine refactored use internal state instead DOM scanning. changes R API — existing code works without modification. Added scroll containers long option lists, debounced search, .gt-loading / .gt-disabled CSS utility classes.

title: glasstabs 0.2.1
text: Added dedicated cheatsheet vignette common widget patterns server-side helpers. Added smoke-test example app covering programmatic tab switching, dynamic tab visibility, runtime tab insertion/removal, rapid interaction paths. Expanded README pkgdown navigation surface new server-side tab controls cheatsheet clearly.

Added optional label support glassMultiSelect(). Added configurable all_label support control trigger text shown choices selected.

title: glasstabs 0.1.1
text: Aligned glassSelect() glassMultiSelect() closely standard Shiny input behavior. Added client-side input bindings select widgets now support session$sendInputMessage() update patterns. Preserved current glassMultiSelect() default behavior selected = NULL initializes choices selected. Improved choice normalization named choices retain display labels correctly. Improved hue normalization "filled" multi-select style. Updated vignettes examples show direct Shiny input usage server-side update patterns.

title: glasstabs 0.1.0

title: glasstabs 0.1.0
text: glassTabsUI() — animated glassmorphism-style tab navigation sliding glass halo transfer trace tabs. glassTabPanel() — defines single tab button content pane. glassTabsServer() — reactive helper tracks active tab value. glass_tab_theme() — custom color theme glassTabsUI() eight independent handles: tab_text, tab_active_text, halo_bg, halo_border, content_bg, content_border, card_bg, card_text. Built-"dark" (default) "light" presets glassTabsUI(). Keyboard navigation using Left Right arrow keys. Full bs4Dash compatibility via wrap = FALSE.

title: glasstabs 0.1.0
text: glassMultiSelect() — dropdown filter live search, select-indeterminate state, three checkbox indicator styles: "checkbox", "check-", "filled". glassFilterTags() — tag-pill display area stays sync glassMultiSelect() automatically via JavaScript; clicking × pill deselects option. glass_select_theme() — custom color theme four handles: bg_color, border_color, text_color, accent_color. Built-"dark" "light" presets glassMultiSelect(). show_style_switcher, show_select_all, show_clear_all flags hide UI chrome (default TRUE). Per-option HSL hue angles "filled" style via hues argument (auto-assigned omitted).

title: glasstabs 0.1.0
text: useGlassTabs() — injects CSS JavaScript via htmltools::htmlDependency, deduplicated automatically Shiny. theming uses pre-computed CSS variables (color-mix()), ensuring compatibility Shiny’s embedded browser. Multiple instances either widget page work independently; instance scoped id.
